behaviorsAudited: opens a file in supervised embedded Neovim and attaches UI before accepting input
implements save, force save, quit, force quit, dirty close prevention, surface switch cleanup, app exit cleanup, and crash cleanup
tracks dirty state from Neovim buffer options and write events
prevents orphaned Neovim child processes after normal close, parent exit, and test failure
surfaces lifecycle errors in provider status without crashing the whole TUI

edgeCasesAudited: dirty quit returns a refusal status while keeping the provider alive
force quit discards edits and resolves cleanup after Neovim exits
process exit before UI attach rejects open and clears pending requests
double close waits on one cleanup path and does not send duplicate quit commands
startup failure after spawn but before UI attach completes kills the spawned child process
dirty surface switch is refused before BUFFER unmount cleanup can discard embedded Neovim edits
BufferSurface unmount on surface switch cleans up the active provider instead of leaving an embedded Neovim process alive
provider replacement waits for active Neovim cleanup before opening the replacement provider
superseded Neovim open after session start cleans up the stale spawned session
cleanup during in-flight Neovim startup cancels the open and cleans up the late spawned session
cleanup during in-flight Neovim startup ignores a late start failure without publishing stale errors
parent cleanup kills the child process group when Neovim ignores graceful quit

commandResults: PASS: 5 test files passed, 68 tests passed, duration 9.49s
sourceEvidence: TODO.md:66-92 requires supervised embedded Neovim, UI attach, input forwarding, clean shutdown, and no orphaned process behavior.
TODO.md:118-134 requires dirty-state tracking, deterministic file safety, and dirty close/surface-switch prevention.
TODO.md:155-157 scopes buffer-lifecycle to open, save, force-save, quit, force-quit, dirty close prevention, surface switch, app exit, and crash cleanup.
TODO.md:180-210 requires lifecycle cleanup tests and PTY coverage for save, dirty quit refusal, force quit, and no orphaned Neovim processes.
behaviorEvidence: Open waits for read snapshot and startSession before assigning a usable session in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/neovim/NeovimBufferProvider.ts:90-140, and handleInput refuses input without a session at runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/neovim/NeovimBufferProvider.ts:244-258. startEmbeddedNeovim spawns, attaches the UI, edits the file, and installs dirty autocmds before returning a session in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/neovim/NeovimLifecycle.ts:137-173. Tests assert the open options, ready snapshot, terminal grid, pane size, and empty pre-session input refusal in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-provider.contract.test.ts:17-61 and :358-368.

Save, force-save, quit, force-quit, cleanup, and dirty close prevention are implemented in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/neovim/NeovimBufferProvider.ts:148-197 and runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/neovim/NeovimLifecycle.ts:83-134. Surface command routing and unmount cleanup are implemented in runtime/src/tui/workbench/surfaces/BufferSurface.tsx:87-89 and :254-317, dirty surface-switch refusal is implemented in runtime/src/tui/workbench/state.ts:22-64, and parent/app exit cleanup is implemented in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/neovim/NeovimProcess.ts:86-112. Assertions cover save/force-save and dirty close in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-provider.contract.test.ts:119-172, command routing in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-surface.test.tsx:296-327, surface switch and unmount cleanup in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-surface.test.tsx:1431-1610, and real Neovim save/force quit behavior in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-lifecycle.contract.test.ts:226-273.

Dirty state is read from nvim_buf_get_option in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/neovim/NeovimLifecycle.ts:89-93, emitted from TextChanged/BufWrite/FileChanged autocmds in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/neovim/NeovimLifecycle.ts:186-194, and propagated through the provider callbacks and refresh logic in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/neovim/NeovimBufferProvider.ts:120-123 and :299-316. Tests assert dirty true/false notifications and dirty read failure handling in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-provider.contract.test.ts:384-394 and real session dirty/save transitions in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-lifecycle.contract.test.ts:254-265.

No-orphan cleanup is implemented by idempotent session cleanup in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/neovim/NeovimLifecycle.ts:121-134, startup failure cleanup in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/neovim/NeovimLifecycle.ts:155-165, detached process-group termination and tracked parent-exit cleanup in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/neovim/NeovimProcess.ts:19-35 and :42-112, and PTY runner failure cleanup in runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/runner.mjs:173-185. Tests assert cleanup idempotency and timeout kill in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-lifecycle.contract.test.ts:24-98, process-group and parent cleanup in :198-223, real close process death in :267-272, and PTY child cleanup in runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:44-47 and :181-193 plus runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/122-workbench-buffer-neovim-kill-cleanup.mjs:42-47.

Lifecycle errors are surfaced as provider status without throwing through NeovimBufferProvider catch/onError paths in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/neovim/NeovimBufferProvider.ts:124-145, save/focus/resize/click error handlers in :165-170, :264-276, and :280-282, and process stderr error forwarding in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/neovim/NeovimLifecycle.ts:196-213. Tests assert open/read/startup errors, session callback errors, save errors, and post-exit closed status in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-provider.contract.test.ts:216-270 and :374-406, with startup stderr asserted in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-lifecycle.contract.test.ts:148-167.

Force quit discard and cleanup are implemented in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/neovim/NeovimLifecycle.ts:115-134 and runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/neovim/NeovimBufferProvider.ts:188-197, and asserted in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-provider.contract.test.ts:159-164, runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-lifecycle.contract.test.ts:263-272, and runtime/scripts/check-tui-e2e/scenarios/120-workbench-buffer-neovim.mjs:181-193.

Process exit before UI attach is handled by startup cleanup and rethrow in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/neovim/NeovimLifecycle.ts:155-165 and by provider open error/generation handling in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/neovim/NeovimBufferProvider.ts:141-145; assertions cover startup exit/error and recovery in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-lifecycle.contract.test.ts:148-167 and runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-provider.contract.test.ts:260-269.

Double close is serialized by the session quit promise and cleanup promise in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/neovim/NeovimLifecycle.ts:95-105 and :121-124; assertions prove one quit/quit! command for concurrent closes in runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-lifecycle.contract.test.ts:114-145.

Startup failure after spawn kills the live child in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/neovim/NeovimLifecycle.ts:155-165; runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-lifecycle.contract.test.ts:170-196 asserts the spawned process dies and the tracked process count returns to zero.

Dirty surface switching is blocked before unmount cleanup by runtime/src/tui/workbench/state.ts:22-64; runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-surface.test.tsx:1431-1503 asserts the surface remains BUFFER, the approval overlay is set, and provider cleanup is not called.

BufferSurface unmount calls controller cleanup in runtime/src/tui/workbench/surfaces/BufferSurface.tsx:87-89 and the controller performs one active-provider cleanup in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/BufferProviderController.ts:149-166; runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-surface.test.tsx:1505-1610 asserts cleanup starts and is called once.

Provider replacement waits for active provider cleanup in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/BufferProviderController.ts:173-180; runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-provider-boundary.contract.test.ts:225-262 asserts replacement.open is not called until active cleanup resolves.

Superseded open after session start cleans up the stale session in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/neovim/NeovimBufferProvider.ts:134-136; runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-provider.contract.test.ts:272-311 asserts staleSession.cleanup is called once while the active session remains open.

Cleanup during in-flight startup increments the provider generation and resets idle state in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/neovim/NeovimBufferProvider.ts:286-297; the late-resolved session is cleaned by runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/neovim/NeovimBufferProvider.ts:134-136, and runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-provider.contract.test.ts:313-338 asserts the late session cleanup and idle/null snapshot.

Cleanup during in-flight startup ignores late start failure through the stale-generation guard in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/providers/neovim/NeovimBufferProvider.ts:141-144; runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-provider.contract.test.ts:340-355 rejects the pending start after cleanup and asserts providerStatus idle, filePath null, and error null.

Parent cleanup kills ignored graceful quits through detached process-group signaling and forced tracked cleanup in runtime/src/tui/workbench/buffer/neovim/NeovimProcess.ts:19-35, :42-61, and :86-97; runtime/tests/tui/workbench/buffer-neovim-lifecycle.contract.test.ts:211-223 asserts a child ignoring SIGTERM is gone and the tracked process count is zero.
